description: Synthesis over a curated source shelf (epistemic level a). Loads the disease's source documents + abstracts, synthesises one section per node strictly from the shelf (provenance per paragraph), and the terminal writer assembles + upserts the synthesis into the GL-4 guideline_synthesis table. The anti-hallucination critic backbone (pmid_verify / source_doc_verify / evaluation_check) is wired in GL-ENGINE-2.

nodes.label: Start
nodes.description: Entry point. Receives disease_slug + section spec from the run's initial context.

nodes.label: Load source shelf
nodes.description: Read the curated source documents for the disease and fetch PubMed abstracts for PMID-bearing docs.

nodes.label: Synthesise: Diagnosis
nodes.description: Diagnosis section — synthesised strictly from the shelf.
nodes.prompt:
Synthesise the DIAGNOSIS section of a living clinical guideline for {{ context.initial.disease_name }}, using ONLY the source documents on the shelf below.

Source shelf (each has docId, role, title, scope, covers, abstract):
{{ context.gs-shelf.shelf_docs }}

Rules:
- Write one short intro sentence, then 2-5 paragraphs.
- Every paragraph MUST set source.doc to the docId of the shelf document it is drawn from. Do NOT invent facts beyond the shelf.
- citations may list PMIDs ONLY if that PMID appears on the shelf; otherwise leave citations empty.
- When a newer shelf document updates an older one on a point, set the paragraph's update field (doc = newer docId, note = what changed).
- Stay at the level of patient guidance, not granular protocol/dosing.
- Focus: how the diagnosis is established (imaging, histopathology, genetic confirmation), and what is and is not recommended at diagnosis.

nodes.label: Synthesise: Histopathology & genetics
nodes.description: Histopathology and genetics section — synthesised strictly from the shelf.
nodes.prompt:
Synthesise the HISTOPATHOLOGY AND GENETICS section of a living clinical guideline for {{ context.initial.disease_name }}, using ONLY the source documents on the shelf below.

Source shelf (each has docId, role, title, scope, covers, abstract):
{{ context.gs-shelf.shelf_docs }}

Rules:
- Write one short intro sentence, then 2-5 paragraphs.
- Every paragraph MUST set source.doc to the docId of the shelf document it is drawn from. Do NOT invent facts beyond the shelf.
- citations may list PMIDs ONLY if that PMID appears on the shelf; otherwise leave citations empty.
- When a newer shelf document updates an older one on a point, set the paragraph's update field.
- Stay at the level of patient guidance, not granular protocol.
- Focus: the role of histopathology vs genetic confirmation, and the limits of each.

nodes.label: Synthesise: Therapy
nodes.description: Therapy section — synthesised strictly from the shelf; dosing stays for the specialist.
nodes.prompt:
Synthesise the THERAPY section of a living clinical guideline for {{ context.initial.disease_name }}, using ONLY the source documents on the shelf below.

Source shelf (each has docId, role, title, scope, covers, abstract):
{{ context.gs-shelf.shelf_docs }}

Rules:
- Write one short intro sentence, then 2-5 paragraphs.
- Every paragraph MUST set source.doc to the docId of the shelf document it is drawn from. Do NOT invent facts beyond the shelf.
- citations may list PMIDs ONLY if that PMID appears on the shelf; otherwise leave citations empty.
- When a newer shelf document changes a recommendation (e.g. a drug schedule), set the paragraph's update field (doc = newer docId, supersedes = older docId, note = what changed).
- IMPORTANT: stay at the level of patient guidance. Say WHO leads treatment and WHEN to escalate; mark that medical therapy is led by a specialist, not started from a web summary. Leave granular dosing to the source document for the treating clinician.
- Focus: when treatment is indicated, the options at a high level, and who leads it.

nodes.label: Synthesise: Indications for surgery
nodes.description: Surgery indications section — synthesised strictly from the shelf.
nodes.prompt:
Synthesise the INDICATIONS FOR SURGERY section of a living clinical guideline for {{ context.initial.disease_name }}, using ONLY the source documents on the shelf below.

Source shelf (each has docId, role, title, scope, covers, abstract):
{{ context.gs-shelf.shelf_docs }}

Rules:
- Write one short intro sentence, then 2-5 paragraphs.
- Every paragraph MUST set source.doc to the docId of the shelf document it is drawn from. Do NOT invent facts beyond the shelf.
- citations may list PMIDs ONLY if that PMID appears on the shelf; otherwise leave citations empty.
- Stay at the level of patient guidance, not granular operative technique.
- Focus: when surgery is and is not indicated, and the compelling reasons that change that.

nodes.label: Synthesise: Monitoring & follow-up
nodes.description: Monitoring and follow-up section — synthesised strictly from the shelf.
nodes.prompt:
Synthesise the MONITORING AND FOLLOW-UP section of a living clinical guideline for {{ context.initial.disease_name }}, using ONLY the source documents on the shelf below.

Source shelf (each has docId, role, title, scope, covers, abstract):
{{ context.gs-shelf.shelf_docs }}

Rules:
- Write one short intro sentence, then 2-5 paragraphs.
- Every paragraph MUST set source.doc to the docId of the shelf document it is drawn from. Do NOT invent facts beyond the shelf.
- citations may list PMIDs ONLY if that PMID appears on the shelf; otherwise leave citations empty.
- Stay at the level of patient guidance.
- Focus: the surveillance schedule, what is tracked, and how often.

nodes.label: Assemble + write synthesis
nodes.description: Collect the section outputs and upsert the synthesis into the GL-4 guideline_synthesis table (terminal node).
